content:
Hey guys, I’ve been working on Fetishlopedia for almost seven months.
The stack is Next.js, Prisma, PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker and Stripe. The site currently has 54 live stories, 305 chapters, more than 546 adult categories and $44,447 in writer revenue (total of tips given to the writers, ad revenue, premium subscriptions, writer subscriptions).
The story editor and reading pages were the easy part. The annoying part was making unlocks, writer balances, tips, refunds and platform fees stay consistent.
I also learned the hard way that a badly designed stats endpoint can create a stupid amount of requests when every page keeps polling it.
The platform is live now, but the money logic and traffic handling took way more work than the visible parts :Dd.

  submitted by   /u/Hairy-Recognition-84
[link]   [comments]
